steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Apply the triangle angle sum property
steps.content: The sum of all angles in any triangle is 180°. Given angle X = 108° and angle Y = 36°, we write: $$X + Y + Z = 180^{\circ}$$ Substituting the known values: $$108^{\circ} + 36^{\circ} + Z = 180^{\circ}$$

steps.title: Solve for angle Z
steps.content: Subtract the known angles from both sides: $$Z = 180^{\circ} - 108^{\circ} - 36^{\circ} = 36^{\circ}$$ Angles Y and Z are both 36°, confirming the problem's condition that two angles are equal. That matches Choice A — 36°.
